commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Before the sun That is, before old age: the effects of which upon all the senses and faculties are described in the following verses, under a variety of figures. (Challoner)
All are exhorted to live well, before death come to deprive them of their senses and all helps: and to continue in expectation of judgment, the signs of which are given, as Matthew xxiv. (Worthington) (St. Jerome)
Rain. One misery succeeds another, the understanding is darkened, and the senses become dull. (Calmet)
The Jews explain ver. 2, 7., of the future distress of their nation under captivity. (St. Jerome) (Haydock)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Before the sun: That is, before old age: the effects of which upon all the senses and faculties are described in the following verses, under a variety of figures.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
